preamble: The shift from [ε] to [h] is a result of extreme vowel reduction and devoicing. In rapid or whispered speech, the oral articulation features are lost, leaving only the glottal friction trace.

examples.note: In highly unstressed positions in rapid or whispered Modern Greek, open-mid vowels can reduce to breathy/glottal fricative. Specific lexical examples not provided; flagged as poorly documented.

sources: Arvaniti, A. (2007). Greek phonetics: The state of the art. Journal of Greek Linguistics, 8(1), 97–208.
